<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/gen-commands.py — do not edit by hand -->
# Command Reference

All Pepper commands exposed via MCP tools. Auto-generated from `tools/pepper-mcp`.

## Summary

**46 tools** available.

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `look` | See what's on screen — all interactive elements with tap commands, plus visible text. |
| `tap` | Tap an element on screen. Specify exactly one of: text, icon, heuristic, or point. |
| `scroll` | Scroll the screen in a direction. Automatically shows screen state after scrolling. |
| `input_text` | Type text into a text field. Automatically shows screen state after input. |
| `navigate` | Navigate to a screen via deep link or tab switch. Shows screen state after navigation. |
| `back` | Go back / dismiss the current screen. Automatically shows screen state after going back. |
| `dismiss` | Dismiss the topmost modal/sheet. Automatically shows screen state after dismissal. |
| `swipe` | Swipe in a direction (like a quick flick, vs scroll which is a slow drag). Shows screen state after. |
| `screen` | Get the current screen name and view controller. |
| `vars_inspect` | Check or change property values at runtime WITHOUT adding print statements or rebuilding. |
| `defaults` | Read and write NSUserDefaults — the app's persistent key-value storage. |
| `clipboard` | Read or write the device clipboard (UIPasteboard). |
| `keychain` | Inspect and modify Keychain items — stored credentials, tokens, secrets. |
| `cookies` | Inspect HTTP cookies from HTTPCookieStorage. |
| `layers` | Inspect the CALayer tree at a screen point. Returns colors, gradients, shadows, transforms. |
| `console` | Capture and read app logs — both print() (stdout) and NSLog (stderr). Start capture first, then check logs. |
| `network` | Monitor HTTP network traffic — see every API call, status code, and response body. Use this to check if data is loading instead of adding print statements. |
| `timeline` | Always-on flight recorder timeline. Captures network requests, console logs, screen transitions, |
| `crash_log` | Fetch recent crash reports for the app. Parses .ips files from DiagnosticReports. |
| `animations` | Scan active animations, trace view movement, or control animation speed. |
| `lifecycle` | Trigger app lifecycle events (background/foreground/memory warning). |
| `heap` | Find live objects, inspect state, and detect memory leaks. |
| `wait_for` | Wait for a condition to be met — element visible, text appears, or screen changes. |
| `wait_idle` | Wait for the app to become idle — no animations, no pending view controller transitions. |
| `scroll_to` | Scroll incrementally until target text appears on screen. Combines scroll + visibility polling. |
| `dismiss_keyboard` | Dismiss the on-screen keyboard by resigning first responder. Shows screen state after. |
| `dialog` | Interact with system dialogs (alerts, permission prompts, share sheets). |
| `toggle` | Toggle a UISwitch or UISegmentedControl by accessibility ID. |
| `read_element` | Read an element's current value, type, and state by accessibility ID. |
| `tree` | Dump UIView hierarchy for deep debugging. Full view tree with class, frame, accessibility info. |
| `push` | Simulate push notifications — deliver, list pending, or clear all. |
| `status` | Get device, app, and Pepper server info — bundle ID, version, port, connections, current screen. |
| `highlight` | Draw a colored border around an element for visual debugging. |
| `orientation` | Get or set device orientation. Omit value to query current orientation. |
| `locale` | Override app locale, look up localized strings, or list available languages. |
| `gesture` | Perform multi-touch gestures — pinch to zoom or rotate. |
| `hook` | Hook ObjC methods at runtime to log invocations. Transparent — original method is called through. |
| `find` | Query on-screen elements using NSPredicate expressions. Native iOS predicate syntax. |
| `flags` | Override feature flags via network response interception. Set a flag, then deploy to apply. |
| `record` | Record the simulator screen. Outputs mp4 (default) or GIF (if output ends in .gif). |
| `raw` | Send any raw command to Pepper. Use for commands not covered by other tools. |
| `simulator` | Control the simulator itself (not the app process) — permissions, GPS, biometrics, and more. |
| `build` | Build the iOS app for simulator. Returns success or structured error messages. |
| `build_device` | Build the iOS app for a physical device, optionally install and launch. |
| `iterate` | The complete edit→build→deploy→verify loop in ONE call. |
| `deploy` | Terminate and relaunch the app with Pepper dylib injection. |

## Observation

### `look`

See what's on screen — all interactive elements with tap commands, plus visible text.
Use raw=true when you need coordinates, frames, or scroll context.
Use visual=true to include a screenshot for visual validation.
Use screenshot_quality='high' + save_screenshot='/tmp/foo.jpg' for PR validation screenshots.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `raw` | bool | — | Return raw JSON instead of formatted summary |
| `visual` | bool | — | Include a simulator screenshot alongside the structured data |
| `screenshot_quality` | str | — | Screenshot quality: 'standard' (70% JPEG) or 'high' (95% JPEG, for PR validation) |
| `save_screenshot` | str | — | Save screenshot to this file path (in addition to returning it) |

### `screen`

Get the current screen name and view controller.

### `read_element`

Read an element's current value, type, and state by accessibility ID.
Returns detailed info: text content, enabled/disabled, selected state, frame, etc.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `element` | str | yes | Accessibility ID of the element to read |

### `tree`

Dump UIView hierarchy for deep debugging. Full view tree with class, frame, accessibility info.
Warning: view tree can be large — use depth limit or scope to a subtree.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `depth` | int | — | Max tree depth (default: 50, max: 50) |
| `element` | str | — | Scope to subtree of this accessibility ID |

### `status`

Get device, app, and Pepper server info — bundle ID, version, port, connections, current screen.
Add memory=true for process memory stats, or memory_detail=true for full VM breakdown.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `memory` | bool | — | Include process memory stats (resident size, virtual size, footprint) |
| `memory_detail` | bool | — | Include detailed VM breakdown (internal, compressed, purgeable) |

### `highlight`

Draw a colored border around an element for visual debugging.
Highlights appear as real UIViews — visible in recordings.
Use clear=true to remove all highlights.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `text` | str | — | Highlight element by text label |
| `frame` | str | — | Highlight a frame: 'x,y,width,height' |
| `color` | str | — | Color name (blue/green/red/yellow/purple) or hex (#ff0000) |
| `label` | str | — | Label text to show on the highlight |
| `duration` | float | — | How long to show in seconds (default: 0.8) |
| `clear` | bool | — | Clear all highlights |

### `find`

Query on-screen elements using NSPredicate expressions. Native iOS predicate syntax.

Available properties:
  label (String), type (String: button/toggle/text/searchField/tab/etc),
  className (String), interactive (Bool), enabled (Bool), hitReachable (Bool),
  visible (Float), heuristic (String), iconName (String), traits ([String]),
  x/y/width/height/centerX/centerY (Double), viewController (String),
  presentationContext (String: root/sheet/modal/navigation)

Examples:
  "label CONTAINS 'Save'"
  "type == 'button' AND hitReachable == true"
  "'selected' IN traits"
  "label LIKE '*Settings*' AND interactive == true"
  "centerY > 400 AND type == 'toggle'"
  "viewController == 'ProfileViewController'"

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `predicate` | str | yes | NSPredicate format string (e.g. \ |
| `action` | str | — | Action: list (default), first, count |
| `limit` | int | — | Max results to return (default: 50) |

## Interaction

### `tap`

Tap an element on screen. Specify exactly one of: text, icon, heuristic, or point.
Add double=true for double-tap, or duration=1.0 for long press.
Automatically shows screen state after the tap so you can verify it worked.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `text` | str | — | Tap element by visible text/label |
| `icon` | str | — | Tap element by icon name (e.g. 'gift-fill-icon') |
| `heuristic` | str | — | Tap element by heuristic (e.g. 'menu_button') |
| `point` | str | — | Tap at coordinates 'x,y' (e.g. '200,400') |
| `double` | bool | — | Double-tap (two rapid taps for zoom, like, etc.) |
| `duration` | float | — | Hold duration in seconds. Use >0.5 for long press. |

### `scroll`

Scroll the screen in a direction. Automatically shows screen state after scrolling.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `direction` | str | yes | Scroll direction: up, down, left, right |
| `amount` | int | — | Scroll amount in points |

### `input_text`

Type text into a text field. Automatically shows screen state after input.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `element_id` | str | yes | Accessibility ID of the text field |
| `value` | str | yes | Text to type |

### `navigate`

Navigate to a screen via deep link or tab switch. Shows screen state after navigation.
Use list_deeplinks=true to see all available destinations.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `deeplink` | str | — | Deep link destination (e.g. 'home', 'settings') |
| `tab` | int | — | Tab index to switch to |
| `list_deeplinks` | bool | — | List all available deep link destinations |
| `category` | str | — | Filter deep link list by category |

### `back`

Go back / dismiss the current screen. Automatically shows screen state after going back.

### `dismiss`

Dismiss the topmost modal/sheet. Automatically shows screen state after dismissal.

### `swipe`

Swipe in a direction (like a quick flick, vs scroll which is a slow drag). Shows screen state after.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `direction` | str | yes | Swipe direction: up, down, left, right |

### `scroll_to`

Scroll incrementally until target text appears on screen. Combines scroll + visibility polling.
Automatically shows screen state after finding the element.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `text` | str | yes | Text to scroll to (scrolls until this text is visible on screen) |
| `direction` | str | — | Scroll direction: up, down, left, right |
| `max_scrolls` | int | — | Max scroll attempts (default: 10) |

### `dismiss_keyboard`

Dismiss the on-screen keyboard by resigning first responder. Shows screen state after.

### `dialog`

Interact with system dialogs (alerts, permission prompts, share sheets).
- list: see all pending dialogs
- current: get the topmost dialog
- dismiss: tap a button on the current dialog
- auto_dismiss: auto-handle permission dialogs (Allow, OK, etc.)
- share_sheet: check if a share sheet is showing
- dismiss_sheet: close the share sheet

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | yes | Action: list, current, dismiss, auto_dismiss, share_sheet, dismiss_sheet |
| `button` | str | — | Button title to tap (for dismiss action) |
| `enabled` | bool | — | Enable/disable auto-dismiss (for auto_dismiss action) |
| `buttons` | str | — | JSON array of button titles for auto-dismiss (e.g. '[\ |

### `toggle`

Toggle a UISwitch or UISegmentedControl by accessibility ID.
For switches: flips on/off. For segments: advances to next or jumps to specified index.
Shows screen state after toggling.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `element` | str | yes | Accessibility ID of the switch/segment to toggle |
| `value` | int | — | Target segment index (for segmented controls) |

### `push`

Simulate push notifications — deliver, list pending, or clear all.
Delivered notifications appear like real remote pushes. Include data payload to test deeplink routing.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | — | Action: deliver (default), pending, clear |
| `title` | str | — | Notification title |
| `body` | str | — | Notification body text |
| `data` | str | — | JSON userInfo payload for deeplink routing (e.g. '{\ |

### `orientation`

Get or set device orientation. Omit value to query current orientation.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `value` | str | — | Target orientation: portrait, landscape_left, landscape_right, portrait_upside_down |

### `gesture`

Perform multi-touch gestures — pinch to zoom or rotate.
For pinch: start_distance > end_distance = zoom out, end_distance > start_distance = zoom in.
For rotate: specify angle in degrees.
Center defaults to screen center — set center_x/center_y to target a specific view.

Known limitation: pinch may not work on views with custom gesture recognizers
(e.g. Google Maps GMSMapView). If pinch doesn't change state, try alternative
approaches: defaults (set a debug zoom key), vars_inspect (mutate a ViewModel
property), or add a temporary debug bridge in app code.

Shows screen state after the gesture.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `type` | str | yes | Gesture type: pinch or rotate |
| `start_distance` | int | — | Starting pinch distance in points (for pinch) |
| `end_distance` | int | — | Ending pinch distance in points (for pinch) |
| `angle` | float | — | Rotation angle in degrees (for rotate) |
| `center_x` | float | — | Center X coordinate (defaults to screen center) |
| `center_y` | float | — | Center Y coordinate (defaults to screen center) |

## Inspection & Debugging

### `vars_inspect`

Check or change property values at runtime WITHOUT adding print statements or rebuilding.
- list: show all tracked ViewModels
- dump: show @Published properties (use this to check state instead of adding logs!)
- mirror: full property dump including private state
- set: mutate a property live (triggers SwiftUI re-render, great for testing theories)
- discover: re-scan for ViewModels

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | yes | Action: list, dump, mirror, set, discover |
| `class_name` | str | — | ViewModel class name (for dump/mirror/set) |
| `path` | str | — | Property path (for set, e.g. 'MyVM.flag') |
| `value` | str | — | Value to set (for set action) |

### `layers`

Inspect the CALayer tree at a screen point. Returns colors, gradients, shadows, transforms.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `point` | str | yes | Screen coordinates 'x,y' to inspect |
| `depth` | int | — | Max layer tree depth |

### `console`

Capture and read app logs — both print() (stdout) and NSLog (stderr). Start capture first, then check logs.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | yes | Action: start, stop, log |
| `filter_text` | str | — | Filter log lines (for log action) |
| `limit` | int | — | Max lines to return (for log action) |

### `network`

Monitor HTTP network traffic — see every API call, status code, and response body. Use this to check if data is loading instead of adding print statements.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | yes | Action: start, stop, log |
| `filter_text` | str | — | Filter by URL pattern (for log action) |
| `limit` | int | — | Max entries to return (for log action) |
| `max_body` | int | — | Max chars per request/response body (default: 4096). Use 0 for unlimited. |

### `timeline`

Always-on flight recorder timeline. Captures network requests, console logs, screen transitions,
and command dispatch into a ring buffer — no setup needed. Query to correlate events when debugging.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | — | Action: query, status, config, clear |
| `limit` | int | — | Max events to return (default 100) |
| `types` | str | — | Comma-separated event types: network, console, screen, command |
| `last_seconds` | int | — | Events from the last N seconds (convenience for since_ms) |
| `since_ms` | int | — | Only events after this epoch ms timestamp |
| `filter_text` | str | — | Filter events by summary substring |
| `buffer_size` | int | — | Set buffer size (for config action) |
| `recording` | bool | — | Enable/disable recording (for config action) |

### `crash_log`

Fetch recent crash reports for the app. Parses .ips files from DiagnosticReports.
Shows exception type, reason, and crashed thread stack trace.
Automatically called when APP CRASHED is detected — use this for on-demand access
or to look further back in time.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `last_n` | int | — | Number of recent crash reports to show (default: 1, max: 5) |
| `seconds` | int | — | Look back this many seconds (default: 300 = 5 minutes) |

### `animations`

Scan active animations, trace view movement, or control animation speed.
action=scan: find all active CAAnimations. action=trace: sample a view's position over time.
action=speed: set global animation speed (0=off, 1=normal, 10=turbo). Omit speed to query current.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | — | Action: scan (default), trace, or speed |
| `point` | str | — | Coordinates 'x,y' to trace (for trace action) |
| `speed` | float | — | Animation speed multiplier for action=speed: 0=disabled, 0.1=slow-mo, 1=normal, 10=turbo |

### `heap`

Find live objects, inspect state, and detect memory leaks.

Discovery actions:
- classes: search loaded ObjC classes by pattern (e.g. 'Manager', 'Service', 'MapView')
- controllers: list all live UIViewControllers (with hierarchy)
- find: locate a singleton instance (tries .shared, .default, .current, etc.)

Inspection actions:
- inspect: full property dump of a found instance (all ObjC properties)
- read: read a specific property via KVC key path. Supports nested paths
  (e.g. class_name='GMSMapView', key_path='camera.zoom'). Read-only — cannot set values.

Leak detection actions:
- snapshot: save current VC instance counts as baseline
- diff: compare current counts to baseline — growing counts indicate retain cycles
- snapshot_clear / snapshot_status: manage the saved baseline

Leak detection workflow: snapshot → navigate to a screen and back 3x → diff.

Related tools: vars_inspect (ViewModel @Published properties — read AND write),
defaults (UserDefaults — persistent config), layers (CALayer visual properties).

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | yes | Action: classes, controllers, find, inspect, read, snapshot, diff, snapshot_clear, snapshot_status |
| `class_name` | str | — | Class name or pattern to search for |
| `pattern` | str | — | Pattern for classes search |
| `key_path` | str | — | KVC key path to read (for 'read' action, e.g. 'camera.zoom') |
| `limit` | int | — | Max results to return |
| `min_growth` | int | — | Min instance growth to report in diff (default: 1) |

### `hook`

Hook ObjC methods at runtime to log invocations. Transparent — original method is called through.

Examples:
  hook action=install class_name=UIViewController method="viewDidAppear:"
  hook action=log hook_id=hook_1 limit=20
  hook action=list
  hook action=remove hook_id=hook_1

Supports: void/object/BOOL return × 0-3 object args, void + 1 BOOL arg.
Covers ~90% of useful targets: lifecycle, delegate, network, analytics methods.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | — | Action: install, remove, remove_all, list, log, clear |
| `class_name` | str | — | ObjC class name (for install, e.g. 'UIViewController') |
| `method` | str | — | ObjC method name (for install, e.g. 'viewDidAppear:') |
| `class_method` | bool | — | Hook class method (+) instead of instance method (-) |
| `hook_id` | str | — | Hook ID (for remove, log, clear) |
| `limit` | int | — | Max log entries to return (default: 50) |

## Data & Configuration

### `defaults`

Read and write NSUserDefaults — the app's persistent key-value storage.
Use this to control app behavior without UI: set debug flags, enable test modes,
bypass onboarding, inject configuration. Many apps read feature toggles, debug
settings, and cached state from UserDefaults.

Actions:
- list: show all keys (optionally filtered by prefix — useful for finding app-specific keys)
- get: read a specific key's value and type
- set: write a value (parsed as JSON — supports string, number, bool, array, dict)
- delete: remove a key

Related tools: vars_inspect (runtime ViewModel state), keychain (stored credentials),
flags (feature flag overrides via network interception).

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | — | Action: list, get, set, delete |
| `key` | str | — | UserDefaults key (for get/set/delete) |
| `value` | str | — | Value to set (JSON: string, number, bool, array, dict) |
| `prefix` | str | — | Filter keys by prefix (for list) |
| `suite` | str | — | UserDefaults suite name (default: standard) |

### `clipboard`

Read or write the device clipboard (UIPasteboard).
- get: read current clipboard contents (string, URL, types)
- set: copy a string to clipboard
- clear: empty the clipboard

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | — | Action: get, set, clear |
| `value` | str | — | String to copy to clipboard (for set) |

### `keychain`

Inspect and modify Keychain items — stored credentials, tokens, secrets.
- list: show all keychain items (service, account, access group)
- get: read a specific item's value
- set: add or update an item
- delete: remove an item by service (+ optional account)
- clear: delete ALL generic password items

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | — | Action: list, get, set, delete, clear |
| `service` | str | — | Keychain service name (for get/set/delete, or filter for list) |
| `account` | str | — | Keychain account (for get/set/delete) |
| `value` | str | — | Value to store (for set) |

### `cookies`

Inspect HTTP cookies from HTTPCookieStorage.
- list: show all cookies (optionally filtered by domain)
- get: get cookies for a specific domain
- delete: remove a cookie by name + domain
- clear: delete all cookies

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | — | Action: list, get, delete, clear |
| `domain` | str | — | Cookie domain filter |
| `name` | str | — | Cookie name (for delete) |

### `locale`

Override app locale, look up localized strings, or list available languages.
Useful for testing localization without changing simulator settings.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | — | Action: current (default), set, reset, lookup, languages |
| `language` | str | — | Language code for set/lookup (e.g. 'es', 'ja') |
| `region` | str | — | Region code for set (e.g. 'JP', 'US') |
| `key` | str | — | Localization key to look up (for lookup action) |

### `flags`

Override feature flags via network response interception. Set a flag, then deploy to apply.

Mechanism: Intercepts the network response that delivers feature flags and modifies the
payload before the app processes it. The app receives already-modified data and stores it
through its normal code path — no timing races, no ivar hacking.

Workflow:
  1. flags action=set key="some_flag" value="true"
  2. deploy  (restarts app — first flag fetch is intercepted with your override)
  3. look visual=true screenshot_quality=high  (verify + capture)

Overrides persist across deploys until explicitly cleared.
- list: show all active overrides + network override status
- get: read a specific flag's override and service value
- set: override a flag (intercepts server flag response on next deploy)
- clear: remove one override (key=...) or all overrides (no key)

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | — | Action: list, get, set, clear |
| `key` | str | — | Feature flag key |
| `value` | str | — | Value to set (true/false for bools, or string/int) |

## Flow Control

### `wait_for`

Wait for a condition to be met — element visible, text appears, or screen changes.
Polls at 100ms intervals until the condition is satisfied or timeout.
Use this after navigation or actions that trigger async UI updates.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `element` | str | — | Accessibility ID of element to wait for |
| `state` | str | — | State to wait for: visible (default), exists, has_value |
| `value` | str | — | Expected value (for has_value state) |
| `text` | str | — | Wait for text to appear on screen (alternative to element) |
| `screen` | str | — | Wait for a specific screen ID (alternative to element) |
| `timeout_ms` | int | — | Timeout in milliseconds (default: 5000) |

### `wait_idle`

Wait for the app to become idle — no animations, no pending view controller transitions.
Use after navigation or complex interactions to ensure the UI has settled before inspecting.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `timeout_ms` | int | — | Timeout in milliseconds (default: 3000) |
| `include_network` | bool | — | Also wait for pending network requests |
| `debug` | bool | — | Return what's blocking idle instead of waiting |

### `record`

Record the simulator screen. Outputs mp4 (default) or GIF (if output ends in .gif).

Start/stop workflow:
  1. record action=start
  2. (do your interactions — tap, scroll, navigate, etc.)
  3. record action=stop output=/tmp/my-recording.mp4

Upload videos to GitHub via Playwright MCP (browser automation) for autoplay in PRs.
Upload GIFs/screenshots via upload-screenshot tool (release assets).

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | yes | Action: start, stop |
| `output` | str | — | Output path for stop (default: /tmp/pepper-recording.mp4). Use .gif extension for GIF output. |
| `fps` | int | — | GIF frame rate, ignored for mp4 (default: 12) |

### `raw`

Send any raw command to Pepper. Use for commands not covered by other tools.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `cmd` | str | yes | Command name |
| `params` | str | — | JSON params string |
| `timeout` | float | — | Timeout in seconds |

## Build & Deploy

### `simulator`

Control the simulator itself (not the app process) — permissions, GPS, biometrics, and more.

Actions:
  list — list simulators with active Pepper connections
  install — install an app from .app/.ipa path
  uninstall — remove an app by bundle ID
  location — set simulated GPS coordinates (latitude + longitude). Clear with latitude=0, longitude=0.
    Use this to test location-dependent features without physical movement.
  permissions — grant/revoke/reset app permissions (requires bundle_id + permission + permission_value).
    Permissions: photos, camera, microphone, contacts, calendar, reminders,
    location-always, location-when-in-use, notifications, health.
  biometrics — enroll Face ID (biometric_type='enroll') or trigger match/fail (biometric_type='match').
    Use to test auth flows that require Face ID.
  privacy_reset — reset ALL privacy permissions for a bundle ID (starts fresh)
  open_url — open a URL (deep link or web) in the simulator. Use for testing deep link routing.
  addmedia — inject a photo or video into the simulator's camera roll (requires media_path).
    Use to test photo pickers, profile image uploads, gallery features, etc.
  boot — boot the simulator (prefer this over raw simctl boot)
  shutdown — shutdown the simulator
  erase — factory reset the simulator (WARNING: destroys all data and apps)
  status_bar — override status bar display (set time='09:41' for screenshots, clear_time=true to reset)

Related tools: deploy (restart app with Pepper), flags (feature flag overrides),
defaults (set app UserDefaults from outside).

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | yes | Action: list, install, uninstall, location, permissions, biometrics, privacy_reset, open_url, addmedia, boot, shutdown, erase, status_bar |
| `simulator_id` | str | — | Simulator UDID (auto-resolved if only one booted) |
| `app_path` | str | — | Path to .app/.ipa for action=install |
| `bundle_id` | str | — | App bundle ID for action=uninstall |
| `latitude` | float | — | Latitude for action=location |
| `longitude` | float | — | Longitude for action=location |
| `permission` | str | — | Permission for action=permissions: photos, camera, microphone, contacts, calendar, reminders, location-always, location-when-in-use, notifications, health |
| `permission_value` | str | — | Permission value: grant, revoke, reset |
| `biometric_type` | str | — | For action=biometrics: enroll or match |
| `url` | str | — | URL for action=open_url (deep link or web) |
| `media_path` | str | — | Path to image/video file for action=addmedia |
| `clear_time` | bool | — | For action=status_bar: clear override instead of setting |
| `time` | str | — | For action=status_bar: time string like '09:41' |

### `build`

Build the iOS app for simulator. Returns success or structured error messages.
Workspace is required — no implicit fallback. Uses worktree-aware DerivedData isolation automatically.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `workspace` | str | yes | Absolute path to the .xcworkspace to build |
| `scheme` | str | — | Build scheme (default: from .env APP_SCHEME) |

### `build_device`

Build the iOS app for a physical device, optionally install and launch.
NO dylib injection — plain device build for testing on real hardware.
Workspace is required — no implicit fallback. Uses worktree-aware DerivedData isolation.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `workspace` | str | yes | Absolute path to the .xcworkspace to build |
| `scheme` | str | — | Build scheme (default: from .env APP_SCHEME) |
| `device` | str | — | Device xcodebuild ID (default: from .env DEVICE_XCODEBUILD_ID) |
| `install` | bool | — | Install on device after building |
| `launch` | bool | — | Launch app after installing |

### `iterate`

The complete edit→build→deploy→verify loop in ONE call.
Builds the app, installs it on the simulator, launches with Pepper injected,
and returns the screen state. Use this after editing code to see your changes.
Workspace is required — no implicit fallback.

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `workspace` | str | yes | Absolute path to the .xcworkspace to build |
| `scheme` | str | — | Build scheme (default: from .env APP_SCHEME) |
| `bundle_id` | str | — | App bundle ID (default: from .env APP_BUNDLE_ID) |

### `deploy`

Terminate and relaunch the app with Pepper dylib injection.
Does NOT rebuild — use `build` or `iterate` for that.
Use this when you just need to restart the app (e.g. after a crash).

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `bundle_id` | str | — | App bundle ID (default: from .env) |
| `dylib_path` | str | — | Path to Pepper dylib (default: from .env) |

## Other

### `lifecycle`

Trigger app lifecycle events (background/foreground/memory warning).

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `action` | str | yes | Action: background, foreground, memory_warning |

## WebSocket Protocol

Commands can also be sent directly via WebSocket to `ws://localhost:<port>`.

```json
{"id": "abc123", "cmd": "command_name", "params": {...}}
```

Response: `{"id": "abc123", "status": "ok", "data": {...}}`
